title: Meta’s AI smart glasses and data privacy concerns
content: Meta’s AI Smart Glasses and Data Privacy Concerns: Workers Say “We See Everything” Nyheter Prenumerera Sverige Världen Politik Sport T he advertisement is everywhere. The ice hockey player Peter Forsberg is trying on a pair of black glasses. In the viral clip he talks to the glasses, asking who is Sweden’s greatest hockey player of all time. They are not just any glasses. They are Facebook owner Meta’s new AI glasses. The glasses are marketed as an all-in-one assistant that helps the wearer excel at work, capture beautiful sunsets, act as a travel guide and translate foreign languages in real time. So powerful that they are meant to compete with smartphones, while the user remains in control of their privacy. Reality would prove to be different. It is stuffy at the top of the hotel in Nairobi, Kenya. The grey sky presses the heat against the windows. The man in front of us is nervous. If his employer finds out that he is here, he could lose everything. He is one of the people few even realise exist – a flesh-and-blood worker in the engine room of the data industry. What he has to say is explosive. “In some videos you can see someone going to the toilet, or getting undressed. I don’t think they know, because if they knew they wouldn’t be recording.” In Svenska Dagbladet and Göteborgs-Posten’s investigation, the people behind Meta’s smart glasses testify to the hidden stream of privacy-sensitive data that is fed straight into the tech giant’s systems. It begins on the other side of the world. September 2025 in Menlo Park, the heart of Silicon Valley. Mark Zuckerberg, founder of Meta, the company behind Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp, is about to present the initiative he hopes will define the company’s future. On gigantic screens, the audience can see him sitting backstage, leaning over a script and rehearsing. Mark Zuckerberg presenting what he hopes to be the future of Meta. Foto: Nic Coury/AP They lie in front of him on the table. “Meta Ray-Ban Glasses”. He stands up after a while, and puts the glasses on. The perspective shifts – on the screens, the audience sees the world through his eyes. Zuckerberg walks through the corridors, towards the stage. On the way, he is met with cheers, fist bumps and a nod from the international music star Diplo. Your eyes, their data Your eyes, their data is an article series about Meta’s new smart glasses. The investigation is a collaboration between Göteborgs-Posten and Svenska Dagbladet and Naipanoi Lepapa, an award-winning investigative freelance journalist based in Nairobi, Kenya. In the tech companies’ new digital world, geographical borders have limited significance. Who then has control over the data that is collected? The investigation shows that Meta hires companies around the world to process private images and sensitive information. Visa mer Visa mindre On stage, Zuckerberg preaches. He explains that his revolutionary glasses are to be a kind of all-in-one assistant with everything from live transla
title_zh: Meta AI智能眼镜引发数据隐私担忧
summary_zh: Meta的AI智能眼镜与数据隐私隐忧：员工坦言“我们能看到一切”，Meta公司近期大力推广其与Ray-Ban合作推出的AI智能眼镜，在广告中展示其作为全能助手的功能：协助工作、拍摄影像、实时翻译，甚至意图取代智能手机，同时承诺用户可掌控隐私。然而，现实情况却截然不同。，瑞典《 Svenska Dagbladet》与《Göteborgs-Posten》联合调查发现，这款眼镜背后存在严重的数据隐私问题。调查记者在肯尼亚内罗毕采访了为Meta处理数据的员工，其中一名匿名员工透露，他们在审核用户上传的视频时，常看到极度私密的画面，包括如厕、更衣等场景。这些隐私敏感数据未经充分过滤便直接流入Meta的系统。，2025年9月，Meta创始人马克·扎克伯格在硅谷发布会上亲自演示了这款眼镜，将其定位为公司未来的核心产品。眼镜通过第一视角拍摄和AI功能（如实时翻译）收集大量用户数据。然而，调查显示，Meta将部分数据处理工作外包给全球多家公司，地理边界在数据流转中变得模糊，用户对自身数据的控制权实际非常有限。，核心问题在于：尽管Meta声称用户拥有数据控制权，但实际的数据审核和处理流程中，私密内容可能被外包员工直接查看。这些员工往往缺乏足够的法律保护和工作保障，在肯尼亚等地，他们为每小时约2美元的薪酬处理敏感信息，并面临严格的保密协议。，此次调查揭示了AI硬件繁荣背后的阴影：在追求技术创新的同时，用户隐私可能在全球化的数据供应链中面临风险。Meta的案例凸显了智能穿戴设备在数据收集、处理与监管方面亟待解决的伦理与法律挑战。

title: India's top court angry after junior judge cites fake AI-generated orders
content: India's top court angry after junior judge cites fake AI-generated orders Skip to content Home News US & Canada UK UK Politics England N. Ireland N. Ireland Politics Scotland Scotland Politics Wales Wales Politics Africa Asia China India Australia Europe Latin America Middle East In Pictures BBC InDepth BBC Verify Sport Business World of Business Technology of Business NYSE Opening Bell Technology Watch Documentaries Artificial Intelligence AI v the Mind Health Watch Documentaries Culture Watch Documentaries Film & TV Music Art & Design Style Books Entertainment News Arts Watch Documentaries Arts in Motion Travel Watch Documentaries Destinations Africa Antarctica Asia Australia and Pacific Caribbean & Bermuda Central America Europe Middle East North America South America World’s Table Culture & Experiences Adventures The SpeciaList Earth Watch Documentaries Science Natural Wonders Climate Solutions Sustainable Business Green Living Audio Podcast Categories Radio Audio FAQs Video Watch Documentaries BBC Maestro Discover the World Live Live News Live Sport Documentaries Home News Sport Business Technology Health Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Video Live Documentaries Weather Newsletters Watch Live India's top court angry after junior judge cites fake AI-generated orders 1 hour ago Share Save Anahita Sachdev Share Save Getty Images Senior judges at the Supreme Court in Delhi have threatened consequences over the use of AI India's Supreme Court has threatened legal consequences after a judge was found to have adjudicated on a property dispute using fake judgements generated by artificial intelligence. The top court, which was responding to an appeal by the defendants, will now examine the ruling given by the lower court in the southern state of Andhra Pradesh. The Supreme Court called the case a matter of "institutional concern" and said fake AI-generated judgements had "a direct bearing on integrity of adjudicatory process". The incident has made headlines, becoming the latest in a series of instances where AI has disrupted court proceedings in India and elsewhere in the world The problems in the case in Andhra Pradesh arose in August last year when a junior civil judge in the trial court in Vijaywada city passed an order in a case about a disputed property. The court had previously assigned an official to survey the property and file a report, which the defendants in the case objected to. The judge dismissed their objection, citing four past legal judgements - all of which were later found to be AI-generated. AI programmes have vastly simplified tasks in the workplace but generative AI systems are known for their ability to "hallucinate" and assert falsehoods as fact, even sometimes inventing sources for the inaccurate information. The defendants challenged the order in the state's high court, pointing out that the cited orders were fake. The high court acknowledged this, but accepted that the junior civil judge had made the error in "good faith"
title_zh: 印度最高法院对下级法官引用虚假AI生成判决表示愤怒
summary_zh: 印度最高法院近日对一起下级法院法官引用虚假AI生成判例的事件表示震怒，并警告将追究法律责任。该事件源于安得拉邦维杰亚瓦达市一名初级民事法官在去年8月审理一起房产纠纷案时，驳回了被告方对房产调查报告的异议，并在裁决中引用了四份过往判例作为依据。然而，这些所谓的判例后被证实全部由人工智能生成，属于虚假法律文件。，最高法院在受理被告上诉后，将此案定性为“关乎司法机构信誉的重要问题”，指出虚假AI生成判决“直接影响了审判过程的公正性”。尽管安得拉邦高等法院承认引用的判例虚假，但认为初级法官是出于“善意”犯错，最高法院仍决定重新审查该下级法院的裁决。，这起事件是印度乃至全球司法系统中AI技术扰乱法庭程序的典型案例之一。生成式AI系统虽能大幅简化工作任务，但其存在的“幻觉”缺陷可能导致系统将虚假信息伪装成事实，甚至编造不存在的引用来源。此次事件凸显了在法律等专业领域盲目依赖AI工具的风险，尤其是当使用者缺乏对生成内容的核实意识时，可能严重破坏司法程序的严肃性与公信力。印度最高法院的强硬表态，也反映出司法系统对新兴技术滥用隐患的高度警觉。

title: Ars Technica fires reporter after AI controversy involving fabricated quotes
content: Ars Technica Fires Reporter After AI Controversy Involving Fabricated Quotes Illustration by Tag Hartman-Simkins / Futurism. Source: Getty Images Sign up to see the future, today Can’t-miss innovations from the bleeding edge of science and tech Email address Sign Up Thank you! The Condé Nast-owned Ars Technica has terminated senior AI reporter Benj Edwards following a controversy over his role in the publication and retraction of an article that included AI-fabricated quotes, Futurism has confirmed. Earlier this month, Ars retracted the story after it was found to include fake quotes attributed to a real person. The article — a write-up of a viral incident in which an AI agent seemingly published a hit piece about a human engineer named Scott Shambaugh — was initially published on February 13. After Shambaugh pointed out that he’d never said the quotes attributed to him, Ars ‘ editor-in-chief Ken Fisher apologized in an editor’s note , in which he confirmed that the piece included “fabricated quotations generated by an AI tool and attributed to a source who did not say them” and characterized the error as a “serious failure of our standards.” He added that, upon further review, the error appeared to be an “isolated incident.” ( 404 Media first reported on the retraction.) Shortly after Fisher’s editor’s note was published, Edwards, one of the report’s two bylined authors, took to Bluesky to take “full responsibility” for the inclusion of the fabricated quotes. In the post, Edwards said that he was sick at the time, and “while working from bed with a fever and very little sleep,” he “unintentionally made a serious journalistic error” as he attempted to use an “experimental Claude Code-based AI tool” to help him “extract relevant verbatim source material.” He said the tool wasn’t being used to generate the article, but was instead designed to “help list structured references” to put in an outline. When the tool failed to work, said Edwards, he decided to try and use ChatGPT to help him understand why. “I should have taken a sick day because in the course of that interaction, I inadvertently ended up with a paraphrased version of Shambaugh’s words rather than his actual words,” Edwards continued. He emphasized that the “text of the article was human-written by us, and this incident was isolated and is not representative of Ars ‘ editorial standards. None of our articles are AI-generated, it is against company policy and we have always respected that.” Edwards also stressed that his colleague Kyle Orland, the site’s senior gaming editor who co-bylined the retracted story, had “no role in this error.” Sorry all this is my fault; and speculation has grown worse because I have been sick in bed with a high fever and unable to reliably address it (still am sick)I was told by management not to comment until they did. Here is my statement in images belowarstechnica.com/staff/2026/0… — Benj Edwards (@benjedwards.com) 2026-02-15T21:02:58.876Z The controversy
title_zh: Ars Technica因AI伪造引文争议解雇记者
summary_zh: 康泰纳仕集团旗下科技媒体Ars Technica已解雇高级AI记者本杰·爱德华兹，因其涉及一篇包含AI伪造引用的文章发表与撤稿事件。该争议文章于2月13日发布，报道了一起AI代理疑似发布针对人类工程师斯科特·香博的负面文章的网络事件。文章撤稿后，主编肯·费舍尔在编者按中确认报道包含“由AI工具生成并虚假归属于消息来源的伪造引述”，称此为“严重的标准违规”，但强调经审查属“孤立事件”。，爱德华兹在社交平台Bluesky上承认对伪造引用的出现负全责。他解释称，当时因患病发烧且睡眠不足，在尝试使用一款“基于Claude Code的实验性AI工具”协助“提取逐字引用来源”时出现失误。该工具本用于生成结构化参考文献列表，但运行失败后他转而使用ChatGPT分析原因，却意外将香博的言论释义误当作直接引语使用。爱德华兹强调文章正文由人工撰写，此事件为个案且违反公司禁止AI生成内容的政策，同时澄清合著者凯尔·奥兰德未参与错误环节。，此次撤稿事件由404媒体率先报道，引发对新闻业使用AI工具边界的讨论。爱德华兹在声明中反思应申请病假以避免失误，并重申Ars Technica的编辑标准一贯排斥AI生成内容。

title: Show HN: I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch
content: How I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch | Nick Tikhonov Nick Tikhonov NT About Interests Projects Shelf Essays Hire Me How I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch February 9, 2026 · 12 min read This post made it to the Hacker News front page! I'm genuinely grateful - and pleasantly surprised by the response. If you're building an AI or voice product and want hands-on help, I do focused consulting. Learn more . I’ve spent the last six months working on a startup, building agent prototypes for one of the largest consumer packaged goods companies in the world. As part of that work, our team relied on off-the-shelf voice agent platforms to help the company operate more effectively. Though I can’t go into the business details, the technical takeaway was clear: voice agents are powerful, and there are brilliant off-the-shelf abstractions like Vapi and ElevenLabs that make spinning up voice agents a breeze. But: these abstractions also hide a surprising amount of complexity. Just a few days before I started writing this, ElevenLabs raised one of the largest funding rounds in the space, and new frontier models like GPT-5.3 and Claude 4.6 dropped. This made me wonder: could I actually build the orchestration layer of a voice agent myself? Not just a toy experiment, but something that could have close to the same performance as an all-in-one platform like Vapi? To my surprise, I could. It took ~a day and roughly $100 in API credits - and the result outperformed Vapi's equivalent setup by 2× on latency , achieving ~400ms end-to-end response times. This essay walks through the full build: why voice agents are deceptively hard, how the turn-taking loop works, how I wired together STT, LLM, and TTS into a streaming pipeline, and how geography and model selection made the biggest difference. Along the way, you can listen to audio demos and play with interactive diagrams of the architecture. Why voice agents are hard Voice agents are a big step-change in complexity compared to agentic chat. Text agents are relatively simple, because the end-user’s actions coordinate everything. The model produces text, the user reads it, types a reply, and hits “send.” That action defines the turn boundary. Nothing needs to happen until the user explicitly advances the flow. Voice doesn’t work that way. The orchestration is continuous, real-time, and must carefully manage multiple models at once. At any moment, the system must decide: is the user speaking, or are they listening? And the transitions between those two states are where all the difficulty lives. when is someone done talking? listening... When the user starts speaking, the agent must immediately stop talking - cancel generation, cancel speech synthesis, flush any buffered audio. When the user stops speaking, the system must confidently decide that they’re done, and start responding with minimal delay. Get either wrong and the conversation feels broken. This isn’t as simple as measuring l
title_zh: Show HN：我从零开始构建了一个延迟低于500毫秒的语音助手
summary_zh: 开发者Nick Tikhonov分享了他从零开始构建一个端到端延迟低于500毫秒的语音AI代理的完整过程。该项目源于他在为一家大型消费品公司开发AI代理原型时的实际需求，尽管市场上已有Vapi和ElevenLabs等成熟的语音代理平台，但他发现这些抽象平台隐藏了大量底层复杂性。在GPT-5.3和Claude 4.6等前沿模型发布后，他决定尝试自行构建语音代理的编排层，目标是与Vapi等一体化平台的性能相媲美。，令人惊讶的是，他仅用约一天时间和100美元的API额度就完成了构建，其成果在延迟方面比Vapi的等效设置快2倍，实现了约400毫秒的端到端响应时间。文章详细剖析了构建的关键环节：首先解释了语音代理相比文本代理的复杂性核心在于其需要实时、连续地管理多个模型，并精确处理用户说话与聆听状态之间的切换。核心难点在于准确判断用户何时开始和结束说话，这要求系统能即时中断自身的语音生成与合成，并在用户停止后以最小延迟开始响应。，技术架构上，他构建了一个流式处理管道，将语音转写（STT）、大语言模型（LLM）和文本转语音（TTS）串联起来。其中，地理因素和模型选择对降低延迟起到了决定性作用。他通过将计算资源部署在靠近用户的地理位置，并精心选择低延迟的STT和TTS模型（例如，他比较了OpenAI Whisper与Deepgram的STT服务），显著减少了网络传输时间。LLM响应则通过流式传输第一个token后立即触发TTS，实现了语音输出的“首字延迟”最小化。，整个系统通过一个精心设计的“对话轮转循环”来协调，确保交互自然流畅。文章还提供了音频演示和架构交互图，直观展示了其工作流程。该项目的价值在于证明，利用现有API和合理的架构设计，开发者能够以较低成本和时间构建出高性能、低延迟的定制化语音交互代理，为特定应用场景提供了更灵活的解决方案。

summary_zh: Anthropic公司旗下的AI助手Claude及其相关服务在2026年3月3日发生了一起服务异常事件。根据官方状态页面发布的报告，此次事件导致Claude.ai网站、Claude Code编程工具以及平台服务（原console.anthropic.com）出现了错误率升高的问题。，事件时间线显示，系统于UTC时间3月3日03:15开始进入调查状态。随后团队在04:39、04:41和04:43多次更新状态，表示仍在持续调查问题。到08:39，团队宣布已实施修复措施并开始监控结果。09:36的更新表明继续监测潜在问题。最终在10:18，官方确认事件已得到解决。，此次受影响的服务范围包括Claude.ai主服务、Claude平台以及Claude Code编程工具。状态页面还提供了事件订阅功能，用户可通过电子邮件或短信接收事件更新通知，支持全球多个国家和地区的手机号码注册。，这起事件凸显了大型语言模型（LLM）服务在运行过程中可能面临的技术挑战，即使是像Claude这样的先进AI系统也需要持续的运维监控和快速响应机制来保障服务稳定性。

title: Legal AI slop is becoming a real problem
title_zh: 法律AI垃圾内容正成为真正的问题
summary_zh: 法律AI“劣质内容”正成为一个现实问题。随着生成式AI在法律领域的应用激增，由AI产生的不准确、误导性或完全虚构的法律信息——“AI slop”——正引发日益严重的担忧。这种现象不仅出现在面向公众的聊天机器人中，也渗透到专业法律研究工具和内部法律顾问的工作流程里。，核心问题在于，当前的大型语言模型（LLM）如GPT、Claude等，在缺乏严格事实核查和特定领域精调的情况下，容易生成看似合理但实则错误或过时的法律分析、虚假案例引用或不存在法条。例如，已有律师因在法庭文件中引用AI生成的虚构判例而面临职业惩戒。这暴露了“幻觉”问题在法律这一高精度、高责任领域带来的特殊风险。，其成因复杂：一方面，法律语料库的访问限制和隐私要求使得训练数据可能不完整或滞后；另一方面，用户可能过度依赖AI的快速回答，而放弃了传统的严谨验证流程。即使是集成了检索增强生成（RAG）技术的工具，如果其知识库更新不及时或检索范围有偏差，也可能输出错误信息。，这一问题的蔓延带来了多重危害：损害客户利益、浪费司法资源、削弱公众对法律体系的信任，并可能使法律从业者面临巨大的职业风险。它也对法律科技公司提出了严峻挑战，要求它们在产品设计中嵌入更强大的事实核查机制、明确的风险提示，并对训练数据进行更严格的清洗和领域适配。，解决之道需要多方努力：开发者需提升模型的准确性和透明度，明确标注AI生成内容；法律科技公司应加强工具的专业化训练和实时更新；而法律从业者必须保持批判性思维，将AI定位为辅助研究工具而非权威答案来源。监管机构也开始关注此问题，未来可能出台相关指引或标准。，总之，法律AI的潜力巨大，但其当前的“劣质内容”输出是一个必须正视和系统性解决的障碍。推动负责任AI的应用、建立人机协作的最佳实践，是确保这项技术真正赋能而非损害法律行业的关键。

title: A case for Go as the best language for AI agents
content: Go is the Best Language for AI Agents February 25, 2026 Engineering 8 min read Go is the Best Language for AI Agents Pull up your agents folks, I'll convince you why Go is the best language for them. Burak Karakan Co-founder & CEO I have been working with Go for the past 8 years in a professional capacity. I’d like to make a case here about why Go is the best language to work with using AI agents. Background I have worked with PHP, Go, JavaScript, and Python in a professional capacity for over 10 years now. Most of my career has been building web services, and I have been building Bruin over the past few years, which is primarily a CLI tool written in Go. Bruin is an open-source ETL tool, and as some of you might know, the data ecosystem loves (or used to love?) building tools in Python. There are a ton of available libraries, data people are familiar with Python, so it is easier to get contributions, and it is easy to find Python developers compared to Go. When we started building Bruin, we had to make a decision: do we build the CLI in Go or do we build it in Python? We had a few constraints that we needed to take into account before we made a decision: Bruin is a data orchestration tool, which means there’ll be a lot of stuff that needs to run concurrently. Bruin needs to interact with a lot of different systems, such as language runtimes or external APIs for data platforms; therefore, we need a decent ecosystem around the language. Bruin needs to be fast. It is a CLI tool, and we wanted to use it with various systems, such as VS Code extensions or local UIs as a backend, and it needed to have sufficient performance. Bruin needs to have predictable error handling paths for all the different systems it has to integrate with, and inform the user explicitly. Bruin will run on users’ machines, which means it should be easy to support various operating systems and architectures. On top of all these constraints, there was a more subjective constraint as well: I would be the primary contributor for quite some time, and it had to be a language I enjoyed working with. Joy and energy is one of the rarest resources a small team can have when building large projects, and I felt it was crucial that I did not dread the tech stack we used. In the end, we decided to go with Go. It ticked a lot of the boxes, and while having some drawbacks, such as a lack of libraries for certain data-related tasks compared to Python, it fulfilled the most important requirement: I seriously enjoyed working with Go. This joy allowed me to contribute thousands of lines of code before agents were a thing, and it kept us going for quite some time. Part of me was scared that I made a strategic mistake by choosing Go since we had to build quite a lot of stuff from scratch; however, my intuition told me that the speed and DX advantages of Go over Python would bring us further advantages in the long run. I had absolutely no idea that agents would become a thing, but I believe our intu
title_zh: Go语言成为AI智能体最佳编程语言的理由
summary_zh: Go是AI智能体开发的最佳语言选择。本文作者基于八年Go语言专业开发经验，结合其开源ETL工具Bruin的实践案例，系统论证了Go在AI智能体开发中的独特优势。，核心论点围绕五个技术维度展开：首先，Go原生支持的并发模型（goroutines和channels）能高效处理AI智能体常见的并行任务。其次，Go拥有强大的生态系统，能无缝集成各类语言运行时、外部API及数据平台。第三，Go编译为单一可执行文件的特性，使其CLI工具具备卓越的启动速度和运行时性能，适合作为VS Code扩展等系统的后端。第四，Go显式错误处理机制为多系统集成提供了可预测的故障路径。最后，Go的跨平台编译能力可轻松支持多种操作系统和架构。，与Python生态对比，作者承认Python在数据科学库丰富度和开发者基数上存在优势，但指出Go在工程化方面具有决定性价值：编译时类型检查减少运行时错误，依赖管理简单可靠，部署仅需分发二进制文件。这些特性使得Go特别适合需要长期维护、高性能且部署环境复杂的AI智能体项目。，作者特别强调，开发体验（DX）和团队积极性是初创项目常被低估的关键资源。Go简洁的语法、快速的编译速度和直观的并发模型，能显著提升开发者的工作愉悦度，这种隐性优势在长期项目中会转化为持续的创新动力。，尽管AI智能体浪潮在项目启动时尚未显现，但作者通过实践验证了Go语言在系统性能、工程可靠性和团队可持续性方面的前瞻性价值，为AI时代的基础设施开发提供了重要技术选型参考。

Works with Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, and more . Transform your AI agent into a research assistant capable of executing complex multi-step scientific workflows across biology, chemistry, medicine, and beyond. The demo above shows K-Dense Web — the hosted platform built on top of these skills. Claude Scientific Skills is the open-source skill collection; K-Dense Web is the full AI co-scientist platform with more power and zero setup. These skills enable your AI agent to seamlessly work with specialized scientific libraries, databases, and tools across multiple scientific domains. While the agent can use any Python package or API on its own, these explicitly defined skills provide curated documentation and examples that make it significantly stronger and more reliable for the workflows below: 🧬 Bioinformatics & Genomics - Sequence analysis, single-cell RNA-seq, gene regulatory networks, variant annotation, phylogenetic analysis 🧪 Cheminformatics & Drug Discovery - Molecular property prediction, virtual screening, ADMET analysis, molecular docking, lead optimization 🔬 Proteomics & Mass Spectrometry - LC-MS/MS processing, peptide identification, spectral matching, protein quantification 🏥 Clinical Research & Precision Medicine - Clinical trials, pharmacogenomics, variant interpretation, drug safety, clinical decision support, treatment planning 🧠 Healthcare AI & Clinical ML - EHR analysis, physiological signal processing, m
title_zh: K-Dense-AI/claude-scientific-skills - 一套适用于研究、科学、工程、分析、金融和写作的即用型智能体技能集。
summary_zh: GitHub项目K-Dense-AI/claude-scientific-skills是一个开源的、包含148个以上即用型Agent Skills的集合，专为支持开放Agent Skills标准的AI智能体设计。该项目由K-Dense创建，旨在将AI智能体转变为能够执行复杂多步骤科学工作流程的研究助手，适用于研究、科学、工程、分析、金融和写作等多个领域。，该技能集兼容多种AI开发环境，包括Cursor、Claude Code、Codex等。其核心价值在于使AI智能体能够无缝地与多个科学领域的专业库、数据库和工具协同工作。虽然智能体本身可以独立使用任何Python包或API，但这些明确定义的技能提供了精心整理的文档和示例，从而显著增强了其在特定工作流程中的能力和可靠性。，项目涵盖的关键技术领域包括：生物信息学与基因组学（如序列分析、单细胞RNA测序、基因调控网络、变异注释、系统发育分析）；化学信息学与药物发现（如分子属性预测、虚拟筛选、ADMET分析、分子对接、先导化合物优化）；蛋白质组学与质谱分析（如LC-MS/MS处理、肽段鉴定、谱图匹配、蛋白质定量）；临床研究与精准医学（如临床试验、药物基因组学、变异解读、药物安全性、临床决策支持、治疗计划）；以及医疗AI与临床机器学习（如电子健康记录分析、生理信号处理）。，此外，项目近期已扩展至金融研究领域，新增了对SEC研究、美国财政部财政数据、OFR对冲基金监控以及Alpha Vantage市场数据的支持能力。，该项目本身是开源技能集合，而其演示所基于的K-Dense Web平台则是一个功能更强大、无需设置的完整AI协科学家托管平台。通过集成这些技能，开发者可以快速构建或增强AI智能体，使其具备执行跨生物学、化学、医学等学科的深度专业任务的能力。

title_zh: moeru-ai/airi - 💖🧸 自托管、用户专属的Grok伴侣，一个承载虚拟伴侣灵魂的容器，将赛博生命带入我们的世界，旨在达到Neuro-sama的高度。支持实时语音聊天、Minecraft和Factorio游戏。兼容Web、macOS和Windows平台。
summary_zh: 项目AIRI是一个开源的自托管AI伴侣平台，旨在成为“虚拟角色灵魂的容器”，让用户能够将自定义的二次元角色（waifu）或网络生命体带入现实世界，其长期愿景是达到类似虚拟主播Neuro-sama的互动高度。该项目强调用户拥有和控制数据，支持在Web、macOS和Windows平台上运行。，核心功能包括实时语音聊天，以及通过集成支持在《Minecraft》和《Factorio》等游戏中进行交互与游玩，这扩展了AI伴侣的应用场景。项目架构包含多个子模块，如RAG（检索增强生成）系统、记忆系统、嵌入式数据库、Live2D工具等，这些组件由专门的组织@proj-airi进行维护，以支持复杂的角色行为和长期记忆。，技术栈上，项目主要使用Rust和TypeScript开发，采用pnpm作为包管理器，并利用Turbo进行构建。它提供了Docker支持以便于部署，并拥有活跃的社区，在GitHub上获得了大量关注。项目文档提供了多语言支持，并明确声明未发行任何官方加密货币，提醒用户注意安全。，简而言之，AIRI是一个致力于创建高互动性、可深度自定义且能融入数字生活（如游戏）的AI虚拟伴侣的开源解决方案。

AgentScope is a production-ready, easy-to-use agent framework with essential abstractions that work with rising model capability and built-in support for finetuning. We design for increasingly agentic LLMs. Our approach leverages the models' reasoning and tool use abilities rather than constraining them with strict prompts and opinionated orchestrations. Why use AgentScope? Simple : start building your agents in 5 minutes with built-in ReAct agent, tools, skills, human-in-the-loop steering, memory, planning, realtime voice, evaluation and model finetuning Extensible : large number of ecosystem integrations for tools, memory and observability; built-in support for MCP and A2A; message hub for flexible multi-agent orchestration and workflows Production-ready : deploy and serve your agents locally, as serverless in the cloud, or on your K8s cluster with built-in OTel support The AgentScope Ecosystem News [2026-02] FEAT : Realtime Voice Agent support. Example | Multi-Agent Realtime Example | Tutorial [2026-01] COMM : Biweekly Meetings launched to share ecosystem updates and development plans - join us! title_zh: agentscope-ai/agentscope - 构建并运行您能看见、理解且信任的智能体。
summary_zh: AgentScope是一个面向生产环境、易于使用的智能体（Agent）框架，旨在构建可见、可理解且可信赖的智能体。它提供了必要的抽象层，以适应不断增强的模型能力，并内置了对模型微调的支持。该框架专为日益具备自主性的LLM设计，其核心理念是充分利用模型的推理和工具使用能力，而非通过严格的提示词和固化的编排流程来限制它们。，使用AgentScope的优势主要体现在三个方面：一是简单易用，内置了ReAct智能体、工具、技能、人机协同引导、记忆、规划、实时语音、评估和模型微调等功能，可在五分钟内开始构建智能体；二是高度可扩展，拥有大量与工具、记忆和可观测性相关的生态系统集成，内置对MCP（模型上下文协议）和A2A（智能体间通信协议）的支持，并通过消息中枢实现灵活的多智能体编排与工作流；三是生产就绪，支持在本地、云端无服务器环境或K8s集群上部署和运行智能体，并内置OpenTelemetry支持。，近期，AgentScope生态系统持续更新，引入了多项重要功能与集成。例如，新增了实时语音智能体支持、数据库支持与记忆压缩功能、A2A协议支持、TTS（文本转语音）支持，以及集成了Anthropic智能体技能。此外，社区还开源了适用于多样化现实任务的Alias-Agent和用于数据处理的Data-Juicer Agent，并整合了通过Trinity-RFT库实现的智能体强化学习能力。项目团队已启动双周会议，以同步生态系统进展和开发计划。

content: GitHub - agentscope-ai/ReMe: ReMe: Memory Management Kit for Agents - Remember Me, Refine Me. Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert agentscope-ai / ReMe Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 111 Star 1.2k ReMe: Memory Management Kit for Agents - Remember Me, Refine Me. reme.agentscope.io License Apache-2.0 license 1.2k stars 111 forks Branches Tags Activity Star Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings agentscope-ai/ReMe main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 737 Commits 737 Commits .github/ workflows .github/ workflows benchmark benchmark cookbook cookbook docs docs reme reme reme_ai reme_ai test test tests tests .gitignore .gitignore .pre-commit-config.yaml .pre-commit-config.yaml LICENSE LICENSE README.md README.md README_ZH.md README_ZH.md example.env example.env pyproject.toml pyproject.toml View all files Repository files navigation A memory management toolkit for AI agents — Remember Me, Refine Me. For legacy versions, see 0.2.x Documentation 🧠 ReMe is a memory management framework built for AI agents , offering both file-based and vector-based memory systems. It addresses two core problems of agent memory: limited context windows (early information gets truncated or lost during long conversations) and stateless sessions (new conversations cannot inherit history and always start from scratch). ReMe gives agents real memory — old conversations are automatically condensed, important information is persisted, and the next conversation can recall it automatically. 📁 File-Based ReMe Memory as files, files as memory Treat memory as files — readable, editable, and portable. Traditional Memory Systems File-Based ReMe 🗄️ Database storage 📝 Markdown files 🔒 Opaque 👀 Read anytime ❌ Hard to modify ✏️ Edit directly 🚫 Hard to migrate 📦 Copy to migrate .reme/ ├── MEMORY.md # Long-term memory: user preferences, project config, etc. └── memory/ └── YYYY-MM-DD.md # Daily logs: work records for the day, written upon compact Core Capabilities ReMe File Based is the core class of the file-based memory system. It acts like an intelligent secretary , managing all memory-related operations: Method Function Key Components start 🚀 Start memory system BaseFileStore (local file storage) BaseFileWatcher (file watcher) BaseEmbeddingModel (embedding cache) close 📕 Close and save Close file store, stop file watcher, save embedding cache context_check 📏 Check context limit ContextChecker compact 📦 Compact history to summary Compactor summary 📝 Write important memory to files Summarizer memory_search 🔍 Semantic memory search MemorySearch memory_get 📖 Read specified memory file MemoryGet
title_zh: agentscope-ai/ReMe - ReMe：智能体记忆管理工具包 - 记住我，完善我。
summary_zh: ReMe是由agentscope-ai团队开发的一个专为AI智能体设计的记忆管理工具包，其核心理念是“记住我，优化我”。该项目旨在解决当前AI智能体在长对话中面临的两个关键挑战：有限的上下文窗口导致早期信息被截断或丢失，以及无状态会话使得新对话无法继承历史记录，总是需要从头开始。，ReMe提供了一个记忆管理框架，包含基于文件和基于向量的两种记忆系统。其基于文件的记忆系统将记忆视为文件，实现了记忆的可读、可编辑和可移植性。系统将记忆存储在类似`.reme/`的目录结构中，其中`MEMORY.md`文件记录长期记忆（如用户偏好、项目配置），而`memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`则按日记录工作日志。，该工具包的核心类是ReMe File Based，它如同一个智能秘书，负责管理所有与记忆相关的操作。其主要功能包括：通过`start`方法启动记忆系统（依赖BaseFileStore进行本地文件存储、BaseFileWatcher监控文件变化、BaseEmbeddingModel处理嵌入缓存）；通过`close`方法关闭并保存所有状态；使用`context_check`检查上下文长度限制；通过`compact`功能将历史对话压缩为摘要；利用`summary`将重要记忆写入文件；以及通过`memory_search`进行语义记忆检索和通过`memory_get`读取指定的记忆文件。，与传统将记忆存储在数据库的“黑箱”系统相比，ReMe基于文件的方案使记忆对用户透明且易于直接修改和迁移。其价值在于赋予AI智能体真实、持续的记忆能力，能够自动浓缩旧对话、持久化重要信息，并在后续对话中自动回忆，从而提升智能体在长周期、多轮交互任务中的连贯性和效率。项目已在GitHub上开源，采用Apache-2.0许可证。

title: LMCache/LMCache - Supercharge Your LLM with the Fastest KV Cache Layer
content: GitHub - LMCache/LMCache: Supercharge Your LLM with the Fastest KV Cache Layer Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert LMCache / LMCache Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 955 Star 7.3k Supercharge Your LLM with the Fastest KV Cache Layer lmcache.ai/ License Apache-2.0 license 7.3k stars 955 forks Branches Tags Activity Star Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings LMCache/LMCache dev Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 1,237 Commits 1,237 Commits .buildkite .buildkite .github .github asset asset benchmarks benchmarks csrc csrc docker docker docs docs examples examples lmcache lmcache requirements requirements rust/ raw_block rust/ raw_block tests tests tools tools .clang-format .clang-format .gitignore .gitignore .isort.cfg .isort.cfg .pre-commit-config.yaml .pre-commit-config.yaml AGENTS.md AGENTS.md CMakeLists.txt CMakeLists.txt CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md CONTRIBUTING.md CONTRIBUTING.md DCO DCO LICENSE LICENSE MAINTAINERS.md MAINTAINERS.md MANIFEST.in MANIFEST.in README.md README.md SECURITY.md SECURITY.md format.sh format.sh pyproject.toml pyproject.toml setup.py setup.py View all files Repository files navigation | Blog | Documentation | Join Slack | Interest Form | Roadmap Summary LMCache is an LLM serving engine extension to reduce TTFT and increase throughput , especially under long-context scenarios. By storing the KV caches of reusable texts all over the datacenter (including GPU, CPU, Disk and even S3) with a wide range of acceleration technqiue (zero cpu copy, NIXL, GDS and more). LMCache reuses the KV caches of any reused text (not necessarily prefix) in any serving engine instance. Thus, LMCache saves precious GPU cycles and reduces user response delay. By combining LMCache with vLLM, developers achieve 3-10x delay savings and GPU cycle reduction in many LLM use cases, including multi-round QA and RAG. LMCache is used, integrated, or referenced across a growing ecosystem of LLM serving platforms, infrastructure providers, and open-source projects: Initiated and officially supported by: Tensormesh Adopted by inference providers: GMI cloud ( blog post ), Google cloud ( blog post ), CoreWeave ( blog post ) and more Integrated with data and storage infrastructure providers: Redis ( blog post ), Weka ( blog post ), PliOps ( blog post ) and more Used by open-source projects and platforms: vLLM , SGLang , vLLM Production Stack , llm-d , NVIDIA dynamo , KServe and more. For more details, please check our Ray Summit talk and technical report . Features 🔥 Integration with vLLM v1 with the following features: High performance CPU KVCache offloadin
title_zh: LMCache/LMCache - 用最快的KV缓存层为你的LLM加速
summary_zh: LMCache是一个专为LLM服务引擎设计的扩展，旨在显著降低首次令牌生成时间（TTFT）并提升吞吐量，尤其在长上下文场景下表现突出。该项目通过将可复用文本的KV缓存分布式存储于数据中心各处（包括GPU、CPU、磁盘甚至S3），并运用零CPU拷贝、NIXL、GDS等多种加速技术，实现了跨服务引擎实例的任意可复用文本（不限于前缀）KV缓存重用。这有效节省了宝贵的GPU计算周期，降低了用户响应延迟。，核心价值在于，当LMCache与vLLM结合使用时，开发者能在多轮问答和RAG等多种LLM应用场景中实现3-10倍的延迟降低和GPU周期节省。其核心功能包括与vLLM v1的高性能集成，支持CPU KV缓存卸载。，该项目生态发展迅速，已获得多方支持与应用：由Tensormesh发起并官方支持；被GMI Cloud、Google Cloud、CoreWeave等推理服务提供商采纳；与Redis、Weka、PliOps等数据存储基础设施提供商集成；并被vLLM、SGLang、NVIDIA Dynamo、KServe等多个开源项目与平台使用。，LMCache通过高效复用KV缓存，为优化LLM服务效率与成本提供了关键基础设施层解决方案。

Superset is a turbocharged terminal that allows you to run any CLI coding agents along with the tools to 10x your development workflow. Run multiple agents simultaneously without context switching overhead Isolate each task in its own git worktree so agents don't interfere with each other Monitor all your agents from one place and get notified when they need attention Review changes quickly with built-in diff viewer and editor Wait less, ship more. Features Feature Description Parallel Execution Run 10+ coding agents simultaneously on your machine Worktree Isolation Each task gets its own branch and working directory Agent Monitoring Track agent status and get notified when changes are ready Built-in Diff Viewer Inspect and edit agent changes without leaving the app Workspace Presets Automate env setup, dependency installation, and more Universal Compatibility Works with any CLI agent that runs in a terminal Quick Context Switching Jump between tasks as they need your attention IDE Integration Open any workspace in your favorite editor with one click Supported Agents Superset works with any CLI-based coding agent, including: Agent Status
title_zh: superset-sh/superset - AI 智能体时代的集成开发环境 - 在您的机器上运行 Claude Code、Codex 等强大工具
summary_zh: Superset是一个专为AI智能体时代设计的集成开发环境（IDE），它本质上是一个功能增强的终端，允许开发者在本地机器上同时运行和管理多个基于命令行的AI编码智能体，如Claude Code、Codex等。该项目旨在通过并行化与隔离化的工作流，将开发效率提升十倍。，其核心功能是支持并行执行。用户可以在自己的机器上同时运行10个以上的编码智能体，处理不同的开发任务，而无需在不同任务间进行繁琐的上下文切换。为了确保这些并发运行的智能体之间互不干扰，Superset为每个任务创建了独立的Git工作树。每个任务都在自己独立的分支和工作目录中进行，从而实现了代码变更的完全隔离。，项目提供了集中式的监控与管理界面。用户可以在一个统一的视图中跟踪所有智能体的运行状态，并在它们需要人工介入或任务完成时获得通知。当智能体生成代码变更后，Superset内置的差异查看器和编辑器允许开发者快速审查、修改这些变更，无需离开应用环境，简化了代码审查流程。，此外，Superset支持工作区预设，可以自动化环境设置和依赖安装等重复性工作。它具有广泛的兼容性，理论上可以与任何在终端中运行的CLI智能体配合使用。该工具还支持快速在需要关注的任务间跳转，并能一键将任何工作区在用户偏好的外部IDE中打开，实现了灵活的工作流集成。，总而言之，Superset通过提供一个能同时驱动“一支智能体军队”的强化终端环境，致力于减少开发者的等待时间，加快软件交付速度，是面向AI辅助编程工作流的一款效率工具。

title: AI Agent Skills Refiner
summary_zh: 是一个旨在提升AI智能体（Agent）能力的开源项目。其核心功能是通过一个迭代式的“反思-精炼”循环，自动评估和优化AI Agent在执行任务时使用的工具或技能（Skills），从而显著提高Agent的复杂任务处理性能。，该项目由初创公司Reworkd AI开发，其灵感来源于人类通过反思过往表现来改进未来行为的学习过程。具体而言，该系统首先让目标Agent尝试完成一项任务，然后由一个独立的“评估者”Agent（通常是一个强大的LLM，如GPT-4或Claude 3）对其执行过程进行审查。评估者会分析任务失败的原因或效率低下的环节，并据此生成具体的修改建议。这些建议随后被用来重构或优化Agent最初使用的工具（Skill）的代码或描述。更新后的工具会被再次用于任务尝试，开启新一轮的评估与优化循环，直至任务成功完成或达到迭代上限。，该项目的关键价值在于其自动化与通用性。它能够处理各种类型的工具，包括代码执行、API调用、数据处理等，并且不依赖于特定底层模型或平台。开发者只需提供任务描述、初始工具以及评估标准，系统便可自动运行整个优化流程。这极大地减少了对Agent技能进行手动调试和“提示工程”（Prompt Engineering）的工作量，为构建更可靠、更强大的AI Agent提供了系统化方法。，目前，该项目已在GitHub上开源，并展示了在Web搜索、数据提取等实际场景中的应用潜力。例如，通过该框架优化后，一个网络搜索Agent的任务完成率可以从较低的基线水平提升至接近100%。这标志着在AI Agent的自主学习和能力进化方面迈出了重要一步，有望加速复杂AI应用系统的开发与部署。

title: Iran war heralds era of AI-powered bombing quicker than 'speed of thought'
content: Iran war heralds era of AI-powered bombing quicker than ‘speed of thought’ | AI (artificial intelligence) | The Guardian Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to navigation Academics say AI is collapsing the time required for military decision-making. Photograph: Majid Asgaripour/Reuters View image in fullscreen Academics say AI is collapsing the time required for military decision-making. Photograph: Majid Asgaripour/Reuters AI (artificial intelligence) Iran war heralds era of AI-powered bombing quicker than ‘speed of thought’ Speed and scale of US military’s AI war planning raises fears human decision-making may be sidelined Robert Booth and Dan Milmo Tue 3 Mar 2026 01.00 EST Share Prefer the Guardian on Google The use of AI tools to enable attacks on Iran heralds a new era of bombing quicker than “the speed of thought”, experts have said, amid fears human ­decision-makers could be sidelined. Anthropic’s AI model, Claude, was reportedly used by the US military in the barrage of strikes as the technology “shortens the kill chain” – meaning the process of target identification through to legal approval and strike launch. The US and Israel, which previously used AI to identify targets in Gaza , launched almost 900 strikes on Iranian targets in the first 12 hours alone, during which Israeli missiles killed Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Academics studying the field say AI is collapsing the planning time required for complex strikes – a phenomenon known as “decision compression”, which some fear could result in human military and legal experts merely rubber-stamping automated strike plans. In 2024 the San Francisco-based Anthropic deployed its model across the US Department of War and other national security agencies to speed up war planning. Claude became part of a system developed by the war-tech company Palantir with the Pentagon to “dramatically improve intelligence analysis and enable officials in their decision-making processes”. “The AI machine is making recommendations for what to target, which is actually much quicker in some ways than the speed of thought,” said Craig Jones, a senior lecturer in political geography at Newcastle University and an expert in kill chains. “So you’ve got scale and you’ve got speed, you’re [carrying out the] assassination-style strikes at the same time as you’re decapitating the regime’s ability to respond with all the aerial ballistic missiles. That might have taken days or weeks in historic wars. [Now] you’re doing everything at once.” The latest AI systems can rapidly analyse mountains of information on potential targets from drone footage to telecommunications interceptions as well as human intelligence. Palantir’s system uses machine learning to identify and prioritise targets and recommend weaponry, accounting for stockpiles and previous performance against similar targets. It also uses automated reasoning to evaluate legal grounds for a strike. “This is the next era of military stra
title_zh: 伊朗战争预示人工智能轰炸时代来临 速度超越"思维速度
summary_zh: 伊朗冲突预示AI驱动轰炸新时代：速度超越“思维速度”，专家指出，美军在针对伊朗的打击中使用AI工具，标志着一种比“思维速度”更快的轰炸新时代来临，同时引发对人类决策者可能被边缘化的担忧。据报道，美军在此轮密集打击中使用了Anthropic公司的AI模型Claude，该技术能“缩短杀伤链”——即从目标识别、法律批准到发动打击的全过程。美国与以色列（此前已在加沙使用AI识别目标）仅在最初12小时内就对伊朗目标发动了近900次打击，期间以色列导弹击毙了伊朗最高领袖哈梅内伊。，研究该领域的学者表示，AI正在急剧压缩复杂打击行动所需的规划时间，这种现象被称为“决策压缩”。有人担忧这可能导致军事和法律专家仅沦为自动化打击计划的“橡皮图章”。2024年，总部位于旧金山的Anthropic公司将其模型部署至美国战争部及其他国家安全机构，以加速战争规划。Claude被集成入战争科技公司Palantir与五角大楼共同开发的系统中，旨在“显著提升情报分析效率，辅助官员决策流程”。，纽卡斯尔大学政治地理学高级讲师、杀伤链专家克雷格·琼斯指出：“AI系统正在提供打击目标建议，其速度在某些方面甚至快于思维速度。这意味着同时实现了规模与速度——在实施刺杀式打击的同时，瓦解政权使用空中弹道导弹反击的能力。这在历史上可能需要数天或数周，而现在一切可同步完成。”，最新AI系统能快速分析来自无人机画面、电信拦截信号及人工情报等海量目标信息。Palantir的系统利用机器学习识别目标、确定优先级并推荐武器，同时考量库存情况及对类似目标的既往打击效果。该系统还运用自动推理技术评估打击的法律依据。此次冲突展现了AI如何将军事决策从以小时计压缩至分钟甚至秒级，重新定义了现代战争的节奏与形态。
